reasoning: ChargePoint (NYSE: CHPT) has clearly not filed for bankruptcy before July 1, 2025. News articles and SEC filings from late 2025 and early 2026 show the company continues to operate normally, trade on the NYSE, and report its regular quarterly financials (e.g., Q3 FY2026 on Dec 4, 2025, and Q4 FY2026 in March 2026). In November 2025, they completed a debt exchange to lower their debt burden. If they had filed for bankruptcy prior to July 2025, their stock would likely have been delisted (trading under a 'Q' ticker symbol), and there would be significant, widespread news coverage of the filing alongside other EV bankruptcies. Instead, only peers like Fisker, Charge Enterprises, and Tritium are reported as having filed for Chapter 11.
